title: Compound inequalities
description: Solve compound linear inequalities. For example, solve 5z+7<27 OR -3zŌēż18.

hints:
###Understanding the type of statement

We are given an $\maroonC{\text{OR}}$ statement. A certain $x$-value is a solution of the statement if it satisfies ***either*** of the inequalities.

Therefore, the solution of this statement is the ***union*** of the solutions of both inequalities. In other words, the entire collection of the solutions of both inequalities forms the solution of this statement.

###Finding the solutions to the two inequalities

The solution of the first inequality,  $2x+3\geq 7$, is $x\geq 2$. [[Ōśā explanation 1]]

The solution of the second inequality,  $2x+9>11$, is $x>1$.

The inequalities $\tealD{x>1}$ and $\purpleD{x\geq 2}$ are graphed below.



[[Ōśā image 1]]

The union of the solutions of both inequalities is $x>1$.

###The answer

The solution is $x>1$.

###Understanding the type of statement

We are given an $\maroonC{\text{OR}}$ statement. A certain $x$-value is a solution of the statement if it satisfies ***either*** of the inequalities.

Therefore, the solution of this statement is the ***union*** of the solutions of both inequalities. In other words, the entire collection of the solutions of both inequalities forms the solution of this statement.

###Finding the solutions to the two inequalities

The solution of the first inequality,  $5x-19\leq 1$, is $x\leq 4$. [[Ōśā explanation 1]]

The solution of the second inequality,  $-4x+3<-6$, is $x>\dfrac94$.

The inequalities $\tealD{x\leq4}$ and $\purpleD{x>\dfrac94}$ are graphed below.



[[Ōśā image 1]]

The union of the solutions of both inequalities is all values of $x$.

###The answer

All values of $x$ are solutions.

###Understanding the type of statement

We are given an $\maroonC{\text{OR}}$ statement. A certain $x$-value is a solution of the statement if it satisfies ***either*** of the inequalities.

Therefore, the solution of this statement is the ***union*** of the solutions of both inequalities. In other words, the entire collection of the solutions of both inequalities forms the solution of this statement.

###Finding the solutions to the two inequalities

The solution of the first inequality,  $12x+7< -11$, is $x< -\dfrac32$. [[Ōśā explanation 1]]

The solution of the second inequality,  $5x-8>40$, is $x>\dfrac{48}{5}$.

The inequalities $\tealD{x<-\dfrac32}$ and $\purpleD{x>\dfrac{48}{5}}$ are graphed below.



[[Ōśā image 1]]

The union of the solutions of both inequalities is all $x$-values  that are less than $-\dfrac32$ along with all $x$-values that are greater than $\dfrac{48}5$. 

This can be represented mathematically as $x<-\dfrac32$ $\text{ OR }$ $x>\dfrac{48}{5}$.

###The answer

The solution is $x<-\dfrac32$ or $x>\dfrac{48}{5}$.

###Understanding the type of statement

We are given an $\maroonC{\text{OR}}$ statement. A certain $x$-value is a solution of the statement if it satisfies ***either*** of the inequalities.

Therefore, the solution of this statement is the ***union*** of the solutions of both inequalities. In other words, the entire collection of the solutions of both inequalities forms the solution of this statement.

###Finding the solutions to the two inequalities

The solution of the first inequality,  $-9x+2> 18$, is $x< -\dfrac{16}{9}$. [[Ōśā explanation 1]]

The solution of the second inequality,  $13x+15\leq-4$, is $x\leq-\dfrac{19}{13}$.

The inequalities $\tealD{x< -\dfrac{16}{9}}$ and $\purpleD{x\leq-\dfrac{19}{13}}$ are graphed below.



[[Ōśā image 1]]

The union of the solutions of both inequalities is $x\leq-\dfrac{19}{13}$.

###The answer

The solution is $x\leq-\dfrac{19}{13}$.

###Understanding the type of statement

We are given an $\maroonC{\text{OR}}$ statement. A certain $x$-value is a solution of the statement if it satisfies ***either*** of the inequalities.

Therefore, the solution of this statement is the ***union*** of the solutions of both inequalities. In other words, the entire collection of the solutions of both inequalities forms the solution of this statement.

###Finding the solutions to the two inequalities

The solution of the first inequality,  $-8x+14\geq 60$, is $x\leq -\dfrac{23}{4}$. [[Ōśā explanation 1]]

The solution of the second inequality,  $-4x+50<58$, is $x>-2$.

The inequalities $\tealD{x\leq -\dfrac{23}{4}}$ and $\purpleD{x>- 2}$ are graphed below.



[[Ōśā image 1]]

The union of the solutions of both inequalities is all $x$-values  that are less than or equal to $-\dfrac{23}4$ along with all $x$-values that are greater than $-2$. 

This can be mathematically represented  as $x\leq -\dfrac{23}{4}$ $\text{ OR }$$x>-2$.

###The answer

The solution is $x\leq -\dfrac{23}{4}$ or $x>-2$.

###The answer

The solution is $x\leq-3$ or $x>7$.

The inequalities $\tealD{x\geq \dfrac{16}{5}}$ and $\purpleD{x\leq-\dfrac{3}{4}}$ are graphed below.



[[Ōśā image 1]]

###The answer

The solution is $x\geq\dfrac{16}{5}$ or $x\leq-\dfrac{3}{4}$.

The inequalities $\tealD{x\geq-\dfrac43}$ and $\purpleD{x\leq-2}$ are graphed below.

The inequalities $\tealD{x>-3}$ and $\purpleD{x<-3}$ are graphed below.



[[Ōśā image 1]]

The union of the solutions of both inequalities is all $x$-values  that are less than $-3$ along with all $x$-values that are greater than $-3$. 

This can be mathematically represented  as $x<-3$ $\text{ OR }$ $x>-3$. 

*We can also think of the solution as all real values of $x$ except for $x=-\textit{3}$.*

###The answer

The solution is $x<-3$ or $x>-3$.

###Understanding the type of statement

We are given an $\maroonC{\text{AND}}$ statement. A certain  $x$-value is a solution of the statement if it satisfies ***both*** of the inequalities.

Therefore, the solution of this statement is the ***intersection*** of the solutions of both inequalities. In other words, the overlap of the solutions of both inequalities is the solution of the statement.

###Finding the solutions to the two inequalities

The solution of the first inequality,  $4x-4<8$, is $x<3$. [[Ōśā explanation 1]]

The solution of the second inequality,  $9x+5>23$, is $x>2$.

The inequalities $\purpleD{x>2}$ and $\tealD{x<3}$ are graphed below.



[[Ōśā image 1]]

The intersection of the solutions of both inequalities is $2<x<3$.

###The answer

The solution is $2<x<3$.
